//: Pro tier ships an aggressive PostToolUse Bash-output truncation hook + a tighter CLAUDE_BASH_MAX_LINES cap. The largest per-turn context overhead /check-context flags is skill descriptions, but the Claude Code schema for skillOverrides requires a per-skill object map (`{"skill-name": "name-only"}`); there is no documented "apply to all skills" form. Users wanting to compress specific noisy skills can add their own object-form override in .claude/settings.local.json.example — the configurator does not ship a default because guessing which skills to compress is project-specific. Also: per code.claude.com/docs/en/settings the setting does not apply to plugin skills at all, further narrowing its usefulness as a tier-wide default.
